issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
issue.errorLine1: return String.format("%.1f %sB", bytes / Math.pow(unit, exp), pre);

issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Calling `setData` after calling `setType` will clear the type: Call `setDataAndType` instead?
Call requires API level 26, or core library desugaring (current min is 19): `java.util.Base64#getUrlEncoder`
Call requires API level 26, or core library desugaring (current min is 19): `java.util.Base64.Encoder#withoutPadding`
Call requires API level 26, or core library desugaring (current min is 19): `java.util.Base64.Encoder#encodeToString`
Call requires API level 26, or core library desugaring (current min is 19): `java.util.Base64#getUrlDecoder`
Call requires API level 26, or core library desugaring (current min is 19): `java.util.Base64.Decoder#decode`
Call requires API level 21 (current min is 19): `android.media.MediaCodec#getOutputBuffer`
Call requires API level 21 (current min is 19): `android.media.MediaCodec#getInputBuffer`
Accessing internal APIs via reflection is not supported and may not work on all devices or in the future
Do not hardcode "`/data/`"; use `Context.getFilesDir().getPath()` instead
Do not hardcode "`/data/`"; use `Context.getFilesDir().getPath()` instead
Do not hardcode "`/data/`"; use `Context.getFilesDir().getPath()` instead
Do not hardcode "`/data/`"; use `Context.getFilesDir().getPath()` instead

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)
Suspicious indentation: This is indented but is not nested under the previous expression (`if (transmitter == t`...)
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`\t` vs `" "`)
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)
issue.errorLine1: Log.v(TAG, "Returned " + offset + "-" + (offset + results.window.getNumRows()) + " rows of " + results.totalRowCount);

issue.message: The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)

Declaring a broadcastreceiver for `android.net.conn.CONNECTIVITY_CHANGE` is deprecated for apps targeting N and higher. In general, apps should not rely on this broadcast and instead use `WorkManager`.
Avoid passing `null` as the view root (needed to resolve layout parameters on the inflated layout's root element)
As of Android 11, this method no longer returns information about all apps; see https://g.co/dev/packagevisibility for details
Expecting `android:screenOrientation="unspecified"` or `"fullSensor"` for this activity so the user can use the application in any orientation and provide a great experience on Chrome OS devices

Fixed screen orientations will be ignored in most cases, starting from Android 16. Android is moving toward a model where apps are expected to adapt to various orientations, display sizes, and aspect ratios.

Use of `scheduleAtFixedRate` is strongly discouraged because it can lead to unexpected behavior when Android processes become cached (tasks may unexpectedly execute hundreds or thousands of times in quick succession when a process changes from cached to uncached); prefer using `schedule`
Use `ResourcesCompat.getDrawable()`
Use `ResourcesCompat.getDrawable()`
Use `ResourcesCompat.getDrawable()`
Use `ResourcesCompat.getDrawable()`
Use `ResourcesCompat.getDrawable()`
Use `ResourcesCompat.getDrawable()`
Use `ResourcesCompat.getDrawable()`
Formatting %d followed by words ("sats"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
issue.errorLine1: <string name="satnet_role_summary_agent">Agents issue vouchers with a stage cap of %1$d sats per voucher and offline-first sharing.</string>

issue.message: Formatting %d followed by words ("sats"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("sats"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
issue.errorLine1: <string name="satnet_agent_stage_limit">This stage allows vouchers up to %1$d sats</string>

issue.message: Formatting %d followed by words ("sats"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("sats"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("Sats"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string

Formatting %d followed by words ("sats"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("expired"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("sats"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("hops"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting string '`adhocconnectmessage`' is not referencing numbered arguments [1]
None of the methods in the added interface (AppInfo) have been annotated with `@android.webkit.JavascriptInterface`; they will not be visible in API 17
Using `getAddress` to get device identifiers is not recommended
Using `getLine1Number` to get device identifiers is not recommended
This `ContentProviderClient` should be freed up after use with `#release()`
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 19
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 19
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 19
Unnecessary; `Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= 11` is never true here
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 19
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `AccountAuthenticator` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `CensorshipResistantRelay` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
This `AsyncTask` class should be static or leaks might occur (anonymous android.os.AsyncTask)
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `SmsRelayClient` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Unconditional layout inflation from view adapter: Should use View Holder pattern (use recycled view passed into this method as the second parameter) for smoother scrolling
This `Handler` class should be static or leaks might occur (anonymous android.os.Handler)
This `Handler` class should be static or leaks might occur (anonymous android.os.Handler)
Use `Long.valueOf(text)` instead
issue.errorLine1: return text == null ? null : new Long(text);

issue.message: Use a `layout_height` of `0dp` instead of `fill_parent` for better performance
Use a `layout_width` of `0dp` instead of `fill_parent` for better performance
Possible overdraw: Root element paints background `@color/satnet_background` with a theme that also paints a background (inferred theme is `@style/Theme_Serval_Satnet`)

issue.message: The resource `R.string.licencebody` appears to be unused
issue.errorLine1:
<string name="licencebody">This software is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0

For the full license text, please visit:
http://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.html

Summary:

This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.

This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.</string>

The image `ic_launcher_call.png` varies significantly in its density-independent (dip) size across the various density versions: drawable-hdpi/ic_launcher_call.png: 48x48 dp (72x72 px), drawable-ldpi/ic_launcher_call.png: 64x64 dp (48x48 px), drawable-mdpi/ic_launcher_call.png: 48x48 dp (48x48 px), drawable-xhdpi/ic_launcher_call.png: 48x48 dp (96x96 px)

Cancel button should be on the left (was "Open | Save | Delete | Cancel", should be "Cancel | Save | Delete | Open")

Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.

issue.message: String literal in `setText` can not be translated. Use Android resources instead.

The project references RTL attributes, but does not explicitly enable or disable RTL support with `android:supportsRtl` in the manifest
